design_philosophy.core_loop: Select unit groups, command them to move or attack enemies, manage squad positioning and unit type matchups across terrain without resource management or base building
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Tactical positioning and unit composition management in real-time combat without the safety net of resource accumulation or base defensibility
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command an elite legion unit fighting a desperate defensive war against overwhelming darkness, executing precise tactical maneuvers rather than managing economy
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately strips away RTS resource management, base building, and tech trees to focus purely on real-time tactical unit control and squad micromanagement. This radical departure from traditional RTS philosophy makes it a 'real-time tactics' game rather than RTS proper.

difficulty.continues_system: Single-player missions appear to have predetermined checkpoint progression with reinforcements arriving at specific points
difficulty.checkpointing: Mission-based checkpointing; units do not regenerate health, creating permanent attrition across mission segments
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Reviewers noted the single-player campaign was 'far too high even on the lowest setting,' suggesting limited difficulty scaling or accessibility options

progression.unlock_mechanism: Campaign progresses through single-player missions featuring the Legion unit, following narrative arc of turning the tide against Balor
replayability_hooks: Multiplayer mode allows control of both Light and Dark units, contrasting single-player Light-only restriction
Different unit composition strategies within fixed army rosters

world_building_techniques: Macro narrative framing: seventeen-year war with fifty-year backstory of Balor's rise
Faction structure: forces of Light (led by The Nine Avatara sorcerers, chief among them Alric) vs. Dark (led by Balor and Fallen Lords lieutenants)
Mission progression following The Legion's campaign to reverse military tide
Named antagonistic leaders with titles (Balor, the Fallen Lords)

notable_bosses: Balor (primary antagonist, leader of the Dark)
The Fallen Lords (group of lieutenants serving Balor)

faction_indicators: Light faction: The Nine (Avatara sorcerers), Alric (chief sorcerer), The Legion (elite unit)
Dark faction: Balor (supreme leader), Fallen Lords (lieutenant commanders)
Territorial control: 'almost the entire land under dominion of the Dark; only one major city and few smaller towns remain under Light control'
danger_signals: Opening context: 'forces of Light on the brink of defeat' at campaign start
Fifty-year escalation of threat (Balor's rise preceding narrative events)
Overwhelming numerical/territorial disadvantage for player faction
environmental_storytelling: Captured or devastated territories held by Dark forces
Remaining strongholds of Light faction (one major city, smaller towns)
Unnamed mythical land serving as contested battlefield showing war's scope
